ucs.title: [LLM] Salt Typhoon / Earth Estries — IIS/Exchange web-shell shell-out (w3wp → cmd/powershell post-ProxyLogon)
ucs.description: Hunts for the Salt Typhoon (a.k.a. Earth Estries / FamousSparrow / GhostEmperor, G1045) initial-access → install → execution chain in which a public-facing Microsoft Exchange or IIS server is exploited (ProxyLogon/ProxyShell-style, T1190) to drop a China Chopper / Demodex loader web shell (T1505.003) that then shells out to cmd.exe, powershell.exe or net.exe under w3wp.exe / MSExchange* worker processes — the canonical Earth Estries telecom-intrusion entry pattern documented by Trend Micro and CrowdStrike.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.process_path) as process_path values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.dest) as dest from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","UMWorkerProcess.exe","MSExchangeMailboxAssistants.exe","MSExchangeFrontEndTransport.exe") Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","hostname.exe","wmic.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.user host | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where NOT match(user,"(?i)(NT AUTHORITY|SYSTEM\\$|HealthMailbox|MSSQL)") | rename parent_process_name as parent | stats min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime values(process_name) as child_procs values(process) as child_cmdlines values(user) as user count by dest parent | where mvcount(child_procs) >= 2 OR count >= 3 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Salt Typhoon / Earth Estries — w3wp/MSExchange worker spawning recon or LOLBin children
// Indicators: China Chopper, SparrowDoor and Demodex loaders pivot from a web-shell context
let WebShellHosts =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe","UMWorkerProcess.exe","MSExchangeMailboxAssistants.exe","MSExchangeFrontEndTransport.exe","MSExchangeHMWorker.exe")
    | where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","hostname.exe","wmic.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","sc.exe","reg.exe","schtasks.exe")
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
    // Earth Estries TTPs — domain trust enumeration, SMB share recon, Cobalt-Strike-like cmd flow
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("whoami","net group","net user","nltest","systeminfo","tasklist","ipconfig","-enc","DownloadString","IEX","Invoke-Expression","hostname","quser")
       or FileName in~ ("certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
              Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              ParentSHA = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
              Child = FileName, ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              ChildSHA = SHA256;
WebShellHosts
// Pivot to file drops by the same w3wp parent — typical Demodex / SparrowDoor / China Chopper artefacts (.aspx, .ashx, .dll in inetpub/exchange wwwroot)
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe","UMWorkerProcess.exe")
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\inetpub\wwwroot\", @"\Exchange Server\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\", @"\ClientAccess\", @"\OAB\", @"\ProgramData\")
    | where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx" or FileName endswith ".asmx"
         or FileName endswith ".dll"  or FileName endswith ".js"   or FileName endswith ".hh"
    | project DeviceName, DropTime = Timestamp, DropPath = FolderPath, DropFile = FileName, DropSHA = SHA256
) on DeviceName
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Salt Typhoon / Earth Estries — Exchange/IIS web-shell shell-out via 4688
let ExchangeParents = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","UMWorkerProcess.exe","MSExchangeMailboxAssistants.exe","MSExchangeFrontEndTransport.exe","MSExchangeHMWorker.exe"]);
let SusChildren = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","hostname.exe","wmic.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","sc.exe","reg.exe","schtasks.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| extend ChildLeaf  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,    "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentLeaf in (ExchangeParents)
| where ChildLeaf  in (SusChildren)
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where SubjectUserName !in~ ("SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE")
| where CommandLine has_any ("whoami","net group","net user","nltest","systeminfo","tasklist","ipconfig","-enc","DownloadString","IEX","Invoke-Expression","hostname","quser")
   or ChildLeaf in ("certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe")
| summarize FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen  = max(TimeGenerated),
            DistinctChildren = dcount(ChildLeaf),
            ChildCmdSample = make_set(CommandLine, 25),
            EventCount = count()
            by Computer, Account, ParentLeaf
| where DistinctChildren >= 2 or EventCount >= 3
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Salt Typhoon / Earth Estries Exchange-IIS Web Shell Shell-Out
id: 8c4b7e1a-1d2f-4d5a-8c1e-saltyphoon0001
status: experimental
description: Detects Salt Typhoon (Earth Estries, FamousSparrow, GhostEmperor, G1045) post-exploitation pattern where a web shell dropped on Exchange/IIS spawns recon binaries or LOLBins from w3wp.exe or MSExchange worker processes.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1045/
  - https://www.trendmicro.com/en_us/research/24/h/earth-estries-attack-on-southeast-asian-government.html
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/13
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - g1045
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\UMWorkerProcess.exe'
      - '\MSExchangeMailboxAssistants.exe'
      - '\MSExchangeFrontEndTransport.exe'
      - '\MSExchangeHMWorker.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\hostname.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  filter_service_acct:
    User|contains:
      - 'NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM'
      - 'NETWORK SERVICE'
      - 'LOCAL SERVICE'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and not filter_machine_acct and not filter_service_acct
fields:
  - Computer
  - ParentImage
  - Image
  - CommandLine
  - User
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate Exchange management scripts run under service accounts (filtered)
  - Health probes spawning hostname/quser (rare; tune via baselining)
level: high
ucs.rationale: This is the documented signature kill-chain Trend Micro and CrowdStrike attribute to Salt Typhoon / Earth Estries / FamousSparrow telecom and government intrusions: ProxyLogon/ProxyShell-style exploitation of a public-facing Exchange/IIS server (T1190) → China Chopper / SparrowDoor / Demodex web-shell (T1505.003) dropped under wwwroot or the Exchange FrontEnd/HttpProxy directories → reconnaissance
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Salt Typhoon / FamousSparrow — SparrowDoor DLL side-loading via signed K7/ESET/legit binary in unusual path
ucs.description: Hunts the Salt Typhoon (FamousSparrow / GhostEmperor) signature persistence + defence-evasion combo (T1574.002 + T1027): drop a legitimately signed third-party binary (historically K7Computing K7SysMon.exe, K7TSecurity.exe, Indusoft 'Indusoft.exe', or VLC/IndusOft hijacks documented by ESET/SentinelOne) into a writable path alongside a malicious side-load DLL (e.g., K7SysMn1.dll, version.dll, mpsvc.dll) that loads SparrowDoor/Deed RAT/SnappyBee. Detects the side-loaded DLL co-located with the signed loader outside its normal Program Files location.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process_path) as process_path values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("K7SysMon.exe","K7TSecurity.exe","K7TSMain.exe","K7TSMngr.exe","Indusoft.exe","vlc.exe","GUP.exe","avp.exe","avgsvc.exe","hpqtra08.exe","goopdate.exe","OneDrive.exe","mscorsvw.exe","BDReinit.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process host | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where NOT match(process_path,"(?i)\\\\Program Files( \(x86\))?\\\\(K7 Computing|VLC|VideoLAN|HP|Microsoft|Notepad\+\+|Bitdefender|ESET|Kaspersky|Google)") AND NOT match(process_path,"(?i)\\\\Windows\\\\System32") | rename process_name as signed_binary | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=t count values(Filesystem.file_path) as dll_path values(Filesystem.file_name) as dll_name values(Filesystem.process_name) as dropper from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name IN ("K7SysMn1.dll","version.dll","mpsvc.dll","libvlc.dll","libvlccore.dll","hpqhvsei.dll","goopdate.dll","profapi.dll","dbghelp.dll","WTSAPI32.dll","OneCoreCommonProxyStub.dll") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.process_name host] | where like(dll_path, process_path . "%") OR like(dll_path, replace(process_path,"\\\\[^\\\\]+$","") . "%") | stats min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime values(signed_binary) as signed_binary values(process_path) as signed_binary_path values(dll_name) as side_loaded_dll values(dll_path) as side_loaded_dll_path values(user) as user count by dest | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Salt Typhoon / FamousSparrow — SparrowDoor DLL side-loading (T1574.002 + T1036.005)
// Pattern: legitimately signed AV/CDN binary (K7Computing is the canonical FamousSparrow loader)
// dropped to a non-vendor path, with a sibling DLL of a name that matches a known side-load target.
let SignedLoaders = dynamic([
    "k7sysmon.exe","k7tsecurity.exe","k7tsmain.exe","k7tsmngr.exe",  // K7Computing — FamousSparrow staple
    "indusoft.exe",                                                  // Earth Estries "Demodex" loader
    "vlc.exe",                                                       // SnappyBee
    "gup.exe",                                                       // Notepad++ updater — Deed RAT
    "hpqtra08.exe","hpqhvind.exe",                                   // HP Digital Imaging — Salt Typhoon SE-Asia ops
    "goopdate.exe","goopdateres.exe",                                // Google Updater abuse
    "avp.exe","avpui.exe",                                           // Kaspersky binary repurposed
    "bdreinit.exe"]);                                                // Bitdefender repurposed
let SideLoadDlls = dynamic([
    "k7sysmn1.dll","version.dll","mpsvc.dll","libvlc.dll","libvlccore.dll",
    "hpqhvsei.dll","goopdate.dll","profapi.dll","dbghelp.dll",
    "wtsapi32.dll","secur32.dll","winmm.dll","onecorecommonproxystub.dll"]);
let LegitVendorPaths = dynamic([
    @"\program files\k7 computing\", @"\program files (x86)\k7 computing\",
    @"\program files\videolan\",      @"\program files (x86)\videolan\",
    @"\program files\notepad++\",     @"\program files (x86)\notepad++\",
    @"\program files\hp\",            @"\program files (x86)\hp\",
    @"\program files\google\",        @"\program files (x86)\google\",
    @"\program files\kaspersky lab\", @"\program files (x86)\kaspersky lab\",
    @"\program files\bitdefender\",   @"\program files (x86)\bitdefender\",
    @"\windows\system32\",            @"\windows\syswow64\"]);
let SuspectExec =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | extend FileLower = tolower(FileName), PathLower = tolower(FolderPath)
    | where FileLower in (SignedLoaders)
    | where not(PathLower has_any (LegitVendorPaths))
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
              SignedBinary = FileName, SignedBinaryPath = FolderPath,
              SignedBinarySHA = SHA256, ProcessCommandLine, ProcessId;
let SuspectDrops =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | extend DllLower = tolower(FileName), DropPathLower = tolower(FolderPath)
    | where DllLower in (SideLoadDlls)
    | where not(DropPathLower has_any (LegitVendorPaths))
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, DroppedDll = FileName, DropPath = FolderPath,
              DroppedDllSHA = SHA256, Dropper = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              DropperCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
SuspectExec
| join kind=inner SuspectDrops on DeviceName
// DLL must sit in the SAME directory as the signed loader — classic side-load layout
| where tolower(DropPath) == tolower(SignedBinaryPath)
| where DropTime between (Timestamp - 1d .. Timestamp + 1d)
| project Timestamp, DropTime, DeviceName, AccountName,
          SignedBinary, SignedBinaryPath, SignedBinarySHA,
          DroppedDll, DroppedDllSHA, Dropper, DropperCmd, ProcessCommandLine
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Salt Typhoon / FamousSparrow SparrowDoor side-loading — ASIM portable variant
let SignedLoaders = dynamic(["k7sysmon.exe","k7tsecurity.exe","k7tsmain.exe","k7tsmngr.exe","indusoft.exe","vlc.exe","gup.exe","hpqtra08.exe","hpqhvind.exe","goopdate.exe","avp.exe","avpui.exe","bdreinit.exe"]);
let SideLoadDlls = dynamic(["k7sysmn1.dll","version.dll","mpsvc.dll","libvlc.dll","libvlccore.dll","hpqhvsei.dll","goopdate.dll","profapi.dll","dbghelp.dll","wtsapi32.dll","secur32.dll","winmm.dll"]);
let LegitVendorPaths = dynamic([@"\program files\k7 computing\",@"\program files (x86)\k7 computing\",@"\program files\videolan\",@"\program files (x86)\videolan\",@"\program files\notepad++\",@"\program files (x86)\notepad++\",@"\program files\hp\",@"\program files (x86)\hp\",@"\program files\google\",@"\program files\kaspersky lab\",@"\program files\bitdefender\",@"\windows\system32\",@"\windows\syswow64\"]);
let SuspectExec =
    ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | extend Leaf = tolower(tostring(split(TargetProcessName,"\\")[-1]))
    | extend PathLower = tolower(TargetProcessName)
    | where Leaf in (SignedLoaders)
    | where not(PathLower has_any (LegitVendorPaths))
    | project ExecTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              SignedBinary = Leaf, SignedBinaryPath = TargetProcessName,
              TargetProcessCommandLine;
let SuspectDrops =
    ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
    | extend Leaf = tolower(TargetFileName), DropPathLower = tolower(TargetFilePath)
    | where Leaf in (SideLoadDlls)
    | where not(DropPathLower has_any (LegitVendorPaths))
    | project DropTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, DroppedDll = TargetFileName,
              DropPath = TargetFilePath, ActingProcessName, TargetFileSHA256;
SuspectExec
| join kind=inner SuspectDrops on DvcHostname
| extend SignedDir = tolower(strcat_array(array_slice(split(SignedBinaryPath,"\\"), 0, -2),"\\")),
         DropDir   = tolower(strcat_array(array_slice(split(DropPath,        "\\"), 0, -2),"\\"))
| where SignedDir == DropDir
| where DropTime between (ExecTime - 1d .. ExecTime + 1d)
| project ExecTime, DropTime, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          SignedBinary, SignedBinaryPath, DroppedDll, TargetFileSHA256,
          ActingProcessName, TargetProcessCommandLine
| order by ExecTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Salt Typhoon / FamousSparrow SparrowDoor DLL Side-Load via Signed Loader Outside Vendor Path
id: 8c4b7e1a-2d3f-4e6b-9c2e-saltyphoon0002
status: experimental
description: Detects Salt Typhoon (FamousSparrow / GhostEmperor / Earth Estries, G1045) defence-evasion + persistence chain — a legitimately signed K7Computing / VLC / Notepad++ / HP / Google updater binary executing from a non-vendor directory, the classic SparrowDoor and Deed RAT side-loading host.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1045/
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2021/09/23/famoussparrow-suspicious-hotel-guest/
  - https://www.sentinelone.com/labs/sparrowdoor-2024-famoussparrow-evolves/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/13
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.t1027
  - g1045
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\K7SysMon.exe'
      - '\K7TSecurity.exe'
      - '\K7TSMain.exe'
      - '\K7TSMngr.exe'
      - '\Indusoft.exe'
      - '\vlc.exe'
      - '\GUP.exe'
      - '\hpqtra08.exe'
      - '\hpqhvind.exe'
      - '\goopdate.exe'
      - '\avp.exe'
      - '\bdreinit.exe'
  filter_legit_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\Program Files\K7 Computing\'
      - '\Program Files (x86)\K7 Computing\'
      - '\Program Files\VideoLAN\'
      - '\Program Files (x86)\VideoLAN\'
      - '\Program Files\Notepad++\'
      - '\Program Files (x86)\Notepad++\'
      - '\Program Files\HP\'
      - '\Program Files (x86)\HP\'
      - '\Program Files\Google\'
      - '\Program Files (x86)\Google\'
      - '\Program Files\Kaspersky Lab\'
      - '\Program Files\Bitdefender\'
      - '\Windows\System32\'
      - '\Windows\SysWOW64\'
  condition: selection_image and not filter_legit_path
fields:
  - Image
  - CommandLine
  - ParentImage
  - User
  - Hashes
falsepositives:
  - Portable / staged software-deployment images (uncommon; baseline first)
  - Sysadmin testing of installers from Downloads / Temp (review parent)
level: high
ucs.rationale: FamousSparrow / Salt Typhoon's hallmark — documented by ESET (2021 hotel-targeting report), Trend Micro (Earth Estries 2024), and SentinelOne (SparrowDoor v2 2024) — is to abuse signed third-party security software binaries (K7Computing K7SysMon.exe is the iconic example, with K7SysMn1.dll as the side-loaded payload that maps SparrowDoor; Indusoft.exe is used in the Demodex telco loader chain) by
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
